articles.title: THE USELESS USECALLBACK
articles.summary: `useCallback` and `useMemo` are often misused, leading to unnecessary complexity and overhead without actual performance gains. This article goes over scenarios where memoization is pointless, such as when passing props to non-memoized components or using props directly as dependencies within `useCallback` or `useEffect`. A real-world example is shown from the Sentry codebase to illustrate how seemingly memoized values can be invalidated by non-memoized dependencies.

articles.title: A FRIENDLY INTRODUCTION TO SVG
articles.summary: SVG is an image format with XML syntax that allows for dynamic manipulation with CSS and JavaScript. It offers UI primitives like circles, rectangles, and polygons, and their attributes can be styled and animated. This post explains presentational attributes like `stroke` and `stroke-dashoffset`, which are useful for creating animated effects like drawing shapes. A key concept explained is the `viewBox` attribute, which creates an internal coordinate system that allows SVGs to scale properly and remain responsive, solving the problem of fixed positioning that occurs when using absolute coordinates.

articles.title: ADDING .MD URLS FOR RAW MARKDOWN CONTENT IN NEXT.JS
articles.summary: It's possible to add ".md" URLs to a Next.js blog to serve raw markdown content. This solution uses Next.js rewrites for efficient URL mapping, directing requests for ".md" files to a dedicated API route. The API route then retrieves the raw markdown content from Content Collections and returns it with appropriate headers, including caching instructions.

articles.title: SIX PRINCIPLES FOR PRODUCTION AI AGENTS
articles.summary: Building production-ready AI agents requires clear and detailed system prompts, strategic context management to avoid overwhelming the model, and carefully designed tools with straightforward interfaces. Robust feedback loops using domain-specific validation are also required to catch errors. LLM-driven error analysis is useful to identify system blind spots.

articles.title: WHEN SOFTWARE ENGINEERS THINK THEY NEED MORE FOCUS TIME
articles.summary: Software engineers often mistakenly prioritize uninterrupted coding time over other important aspects of their job, like collaboration and communication. An engineer's primary responsibility is creating impact and delivering value, not solely writing code. The most valuable work often happens outside the IDE, such as participating in meetings, writing documentation, and helping teammates.

articles.title: FLOWGRAM
articles.summary: FlowGram is a node-based flow building engine for creating workflows with fixed or free layout modes. It is best for visual workflows with clear inputs and outputs, and it aims to integrate AI capabilities.

articles.title: GITMCP
articles.summary: GitMCP is an open-source Model Context Protocol (MCP) server designed to eliminate code hallucinations in AI tools by providing them with access to up-to-date documentation and code from GitHub projects. It supports both specific repository and generic server access, allowing AI assistants like Cursor to fetch relevant information.

articles.title: GO AT AMERICAN EXPRESS TODAY: SEVEN KEY LEARNINGS
articles.summary: Since adopting Go in 2016, American Express has learned multiple lessons that shaped its successful enterprise implementation. It created standardized frameworks and toolkits to support non-functional requirements, used Go's built-in performance tools, and successfully integrated with its observability stack. The company also spent time training internal engineers, leading to nearly a thousand engineers forming a thriving internal Go community.

articles.title: I DESIGNED MY OWN RIDICULOUSLY FAST GAME STREAMING VIDEO CODEC
articles.summary: PyroWave is a fast, low-latency video codec designed for local game streaming. This codec focuses on simplicity, removing motion prediction and entropy coding for speed and error resilience, using intra-only compression and discrete wavelet transforms. Benchmarks show faster encoding and decoding speeds compared to standard codecs like H.264/HEVC/AV1 under similar constrained conditions.

articles.title: CLAUDE CODE WEEKLY RATE LIMITS
articles.summary: Anthropic is introducing weekly rate limits effective August 28 for Claude subscribers that will affect less than 5% of users to address unusual usage.

articles.title: HOW TO MAKE WEBSITES THAT WILL REQUIRE LOTS OF YOUR TIME AND ENERGY
articles.summary: Things like indiscriminate dependency usage, premature framework adoption, and complex compilation steps maximize the time and energy required to build a website.

articles.title: SIGN IN WITH GOOGLE IN CHROME
articles.summary: Google gives Chrome an advantage by displaying "Sign in with Google" prompts differently than other browsers, allowing them to be disabled in Chrome's settings while bypassing extension-based blocking methods.

articles.title: FRONTEND PERFORMANCE CHECKLIST FOR 2025
articles.summary: A large frontend performance checklist for 2025 with actionable strategies for devs to optimize website speed and efficiency across various areas, including HTML, CSS, JavaScript, image handling, videos, fonts, hosting, and quick performance wins.
